about.title: About FireSpreadNet
about.what_text: FireSpreadNet is a deep learning-powered wildfire monitoring and prediction platform. It uses satellite imagery, weather data, and terrain information to predict fire spread patterns up to 24 hours in advance.
about.how_text: The system uses a Physics-Informed Cellular Automata (PI-CCA) neural network trained on Next Day Wildfire Spread data from NOAA/USGS. The model processes 12 input channels including temperature, humidity, wind, vegetation indices, elevation, and historical fire data.
about.data_firms: NASA FIRMS — Active fire detection from VIIRS/MODIS satellites
about.limitations_text: This is a research prototype. Predictions are approximations and should NOT be used for emergency response decisions. The model was trained on US wildfire data and may not generalize to all regions.
about.credits_text: Developed as part of a Machine Learning research project on wildfire prediction. Based on the Next Day Wildfire Spread dataset (Huot et al., 2022).

tips.title: Fire Declared — Emergency Actions
tips.subtitle: A fire has been reported at this location. Take immediate action.
tips.call_emergency_desc: Immediately call 112 (EU), 18 (fire brigade) or local emergency services. Provide your exact GPS coordinates.
tips.evacuate_desc: Leave the area immediately. Move upwind and uphill away from the fire. Do not return until authorities say it is safe.
tips.alert_neighbors_desc: Warn nearby residents and help those who may need assistance evacuating. Use all communication channels available.
tips.no_vehicles_desc: Leave roads clear for emergency vehicles. Only use your car if you cannot leave on foot. Close all windows while driving.
tips.stay_informed_desc: Monitor official alerts via radio, government apps, or local authorities. Follow all instructions from emergency services.
